summary: Added a browser-local Route Replay controller to the Path Map so lane history can play forward as game-like stages while reusing the Event Proof lens.

queries: game UI quest log timeline milestones dashboard design patterns
product roadmap timeline gamification unlock levels UX dashboard
activity timeline replay dashboard UX pattern
pattern_notes: Roadmap and activity surfaces need a compact overview plus explicit access to deeper records.
Gamified dashboards should keep game objectives, win states, feedback, and rewards visible.
Replay-style controls make long histories explorable without forcing a giant first-scan feed.

data_inputs: existing state.pathEventFocusByLane and Event Proof lens
ui_contract: Render Route Replay inside Path Map without adding schema or mutating snapshot data.
Order lane history from oldest to newest so playback feels like a route run.
Provide start, back, play/pause, next, and latest controls.
Keep replay stage changes synchronized with the existing Event Proof lens.
Pause off-screen replay when the operator switches to a different lane.

red_route.evidence: Before implementation, Path Map had 0 .path-route-replay panels, 0 data-path-replay-action controls, and no Route Replay text.

green_route.evidence: Route Replay rendered 1 panel with 5 controls, started at Stage 1 of 64, NEXT advanced to Stage 2 and updated Event Proof to Browser Research with 4 preview lines, latest jumped to Stage 64, and play/pause toggled the playing state.

responsive.evidence: All tested viewports rendered 1 Route Replay panel, 5 controls, Stage 2 after NEXT, an updated Event Proof lens, no bad rectangles, and no console errors.
